title: Intense Flaky Italian Freekeh Pilaf
description: A bitter and adventurous Italian dish from Emilia-Romagna, featuring freekeh and caramelized flavors, friendly to FODMAP diets. This pilaf combines intense flavors for a unique taste experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the freekeh and cook according to package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion until translucent.
Add the garlic-infused oil and cook for an additional minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped radiccio to the skillet and cook until wilted, about 3-4 minutes.
Stir in the caramelized fennel, salt, and black pepper, and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Fluff the cooked freekeh with a fork and stir in the radiccio and fennel mixture.
Transfer the pilaf to a serving dish and top with crumbled Grana Padano cheese.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
